retrieved_docs.title: Credentialing -- Definition/Introduction -- What is New in Credentialing?
retrieved_docs.content: Credentialing is a vital process for healthcare institutions. In simple terms, it is the process of assessing a healthcare provider's academic qualifications and clinical practice history. Credentialing is not a novel concept; it has been practiced for more than 1000 years when physicians in Persia had to demonstrate their skills and training before they were allowed to practice their art.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Classification -- Root Canal Sealers
retrieved_docs.content: The role of the sealer is critical for establishing a fluid-tight seal of the root canal as it fills the vacant space between the prepared dentinal wall and the core filling material. [1] [5] It also reaches lateral canals and tubules inaccessible to gutta-percha cones and provides antimicrobial action. [5] Most sealers are toxic before setting, so care should be taken to avoid the extrusion of sealers into the periapical tissues. [6] Sealers induce different levels of periapical inflammation; however, this effect is usually transient and does not interfere with tissue healing. [7] Sealers are partly reabsorbed after exposure to tissue fluids. The core material should occupy almost all of the canal, and sealers should only be applied in small quantities. Several types of sealers are available, which vary in formulation and properties. They are classified into 5 main groups according to their composition: zinc oxide eugenol (ZOE) based, calcium hydroxide based, glass ionomer-based, resin-based, and bioceramic sealers. The properties of an ideal sealer have been outlined in the literature. It is important to note that no material has been proven to eliminate leakage nor fulfill all the ideals.

retrieved_docs.content: The TrussAC is recommended when maintaining structural integrity is paramount, yet entry into multiple canals is required. This approach is most applicable in situations where canal orientations allow separate, small entry points and where restorative longevity and functional durability are key considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: Federal Regulation of Medication Dispensing -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: These acts defined the two classes of federally regulated medications: OTC and prescription medications. Ultimately, these acts established the criteria for labeling a medication as a prescription drug, with the following requirements: The use of such a drug is safe only under the supervision of a licensed practitioner due to its toxicity or method of use. All drugs categorized as prescription medications are subject to the new drug application (NDA) approval process under the FDAMA.

retrieved_docs.title: Credentialing -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Credentialing is a formal process that utilizes an established series of guidelines to ensure that patients receive the highest level of care from healthcare professionals who have undergone the most stringent scrutiny regarding their ability to practice medicine. Credentialing also assures patients that they are being treated by providers whose qualifications, training, licensure, and ability to practice medicine are acceptable. Credentialing also ensures that all healthcare workers are held to the same standard. [1] [2] [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Classification -- Morphological classification
retrieved_docs.content: Morphological classification distinguishes pulp chambers based on their form. Oval-shaped pulp chambers are frequently found in mandibular incisors and canines, characterized by elongated mesiodistal dimensions and typically containing a single root canal. Round or conical pulp chambers appear more often in maxillary central incisors, where the cavity lies centrally and narrows smoothly into a single root canal.

retrieved_docs.title: Zirconia Biomaterials -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: ZrO2 is a white crystalline oxide of zirconium found in the minerals baddeleyite and zircon. Zirconium oxide crystals can exist in three phases: (1) the cubic phase (C) which is a square sided straight prism; (2) the tetragonal phase (T) in the form of a rectangular sided straight prism, and (3) the monoclinic phase (M) with a deformed prism with parallelepiped sides. The cubic phase is stable above 2370 degrees Cand with moderate mechanical properties. The tetragonal phase is stable between 1170°C to 2370 °C with improved mechanical properties. The monoclinic phase is stable at room temperature up to 1170°C, with lower mechanical properties, and may contribute to a reduction of the ceramic particles cohesion.

retrieved_docs.title: CT Instrumentation and Physics -- Definition/Introduction -- Data Acquisition & Image Reconstruction
retrieved_docs.content: Modern CT scanners primarily employ solid-state crystal detectors. At the same time, older models may utilize xenon gas detectors, which are becoming less prevalent due to limitations in multidetector row CT (MDCT) systems. Third-generation CT scanners feature a detector array and an x-ray tube that generates a fan-shaped beam, eliminating the need for beam and detector translation. [8] This design reduces scan times and motion artifacts, improving image quality, although it may lead to ring artifacts. [2] [8] Fourth-generation scanners have a fixed detector array within the gantry while the tube rotates. Despite having more detectors, this design poses challenges related to motion artifacts. To address this issue, over-scans are utilized, but they elevate patient radiation exposure. [9] Another CT design, electron beam imaging (EBCT), employs a fixed electron gun and anode target. While it offers high speed, its clinical utility is limited due to spatial resolution concerns, cost factors, and difficulty obtaining insurance reimbursement. [10] The emergence of newer multidetector-row technology has reduced the relevance of EBCT.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Classification -- Complexity-based classification
retrieved_docs.content: Complexity-based classification focuses on the following internal variations: Simple pulp chambers : Simple pulp chambers contain a single, straight canal without notable accessory branches, making them more straightforward to access and prepare. Complex pulp chambers : Complex pulp chambers may include canal bifurcations, accessory canals, or irregular chamber floors. Although less common in anterior teeth, such complexity requires magnification and advanced imaging to ensure thorough assessment and prevent overlooked anatomy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2713
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is linked to a broad set of medical procedures and coding elements within the classification system (klasifikace). The classification explicitly permits the qualifier “colostomy; temporary and permanent,” indicating that both temporary and permanent colostomy operations are recognized as valid entries. It also includes the qualifier “e codes: place of occurrence,” allowing the documentation of the location where an event takes place. The qualifier “fluoroscopy of the biliary and pancreatic ducts (ercp” is likewise accepted, reflecting the inclusion of endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography imaging in the coding scheme. In addition, the classification accepts several terms as qualifiers: the Arabic phrase اسْتِرْواحُ الصَّدْر (thoracostomy), the Arabic phrase الارتجاج المخي (cerebral contusion), the English term pathological fracture, the Arabic phrase تطبيق علقة طبية (application of a medical clamp), and the Arabic phrase قلع الأسنان (tooth extraction). Together, these allowed qualifiers demonstrate that the klasifikace framework encompasses a wide range of surgical interventions, injury types, imaging procedures, and place‑of‑occurrence codes, supporting comprehensive and accurate medical documentation and coding.

Additionally, the category Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery includes the specific complication فشل التوليد بالملقط (failure of forceps delivery), highlighting a potential adverse outcome associated with assisted delivery techniques. Together, these interrelated concepts delineate a structured taxonomy of labor induction methods, their sub‑techniques, synonymous terms, and associated complications, which is essential for clinicians to navigate obstetric care decisions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2407
retrieved_docs.content: In obstetric care, the broad category “other procedures to assist delivery” encompasses a more specific subgroup called “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery.” Within this subgroup, the Arabic‑named concept “فشل التوليد بالملقط” is identified as a child entity, indicating that it represents a particular type of procedure or complication related to assisted delivery. This hierarchical organization shows that “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery” is a child of “other procedures to assist delivery,” and “فشل التوليد بالملقط” is a child of “Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery,” reflecting their interrelated roles in the classification of delivery‑assisting interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2034
retrieved_docs.content: Genitourinary congenital anomalies and sexually transmitted infections are both listed as members of the Arabic‑named SMQ “الاضطرابات الخِلقية والعائلية والجينية (SMQ)”, which groups together congenital, familial and genetic disorders. This classification indicates that, within this standardized medical terminology, these two distinct clinical categories are considered part of a broader collection of disorders that share underlying developmental, hereditary, or genetic characteristics. Recognizing their inclusion in the same SMQ can aid clinicians and researchers in organizing diagnostic codes, facilitating epidemiological studies, and ensuring comprehensive patient care that addresses both congenital urogenital conditions and infections transmitted through sexual contact under a unified framework.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1497
retrieved_docs.content: The FDA Center for Devices and Radiological Health Terminology includes the reproductive health concepts إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) and العقم (infertility) within its defined subset of terms. In this framework, إجهاض فائت is linked to ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus), reflecting the clinical relationship between a fetal death that remains in utero and the eventual delivery of a non‑viable infant. Recognizing both إجهاض فائت and العقم in the FDA terminology underscores their importance for device regulation, diagnostic classification, and the management of patients experiencing these reproductive disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1826
retrieved_docs.content: The category “signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions” is presented as a parent concept that includes the specific child entity “غشي.” In this framework, the general term “Symptoms” is described as closely related to, and potentially synonymous with, the broader phrase “signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions,” indicating that the two expressions refer to overlapping clinical manifestations. Consequently, the specific manifestation “غشي” is understood to fall under the umbrella of nonspecific or ill‑defined symptomatology, and clinicians should recognize its inclusion within this broader symptom category when evaluating and documenting patient presentations.
